name: cal-com
description: Manage Cal.com bookings, event types, and availability through the Cal.com API v2

envVars.name: CALCOM_API_KEY
envVars.description: Cal.com API key (starts with cal_ for test mode or cal_live_ for live mode), sent as a Bearer token

tools.name: List Bookings
tools.description: List bookings for the authenticated user with optional status, event type, and attendee filters

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-05-01 for this endpoint

status.description: Filter by booking status: upcoming, recurring, past, cancelled, or unconfirmed

eventTypeId.description: Only return bookings for this event type ID

attendeeEmail.description: Only return bookings with an attendee matching this email address

cursor.description: Pagination cursor: pass pagination.nextCursor from the previous response

tools.name: List Event Types
tools.description: List event types so the agent can find the event type ID needed for slots and bookings

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2024-06-14 for this endpoint

username.description: Username of the user to get event types for

eventSlug.description: Slug of a specific event type to fetch; requires username

orgSlug.description: Organization slug, if the user belongs to an organization

tools.name: Get Available Slots
tools.description: Find available time slots for an event type within a date range, e.g. before creating a booking

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2024-09-04 for this endpoint

start.description: Start of the time range in UTC ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-06-15 or 2026-06-15T09:00:00Z

end.description: End of the time range in UTC ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-06-16

eventTypeId.description: Event type ID to get slots for (alternatively pass eventTypeSlug plus username)

eventTypeSlug.description: Event type slug; must be combined with username

username.description: Username owning the event type, when identifying it by slug

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for the returned slots, e.g. Europe/Rome (defaults to UTC)

duration.description: Slot duration in minutes; defaults to the event type's configured length

tools.name: Create Booking
tools.description: Book a time slot on an event type for an attendee

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-02-25 for this endpoint

start.description: Booking start time in UTC ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-06-15T09:00:00Z (always UTC, regardless of attendee time zone)

eventTypeId.description: ID of the event type to book (find it with list_event_types); required unless eventTypeSlug plus username (or teamSlug) are provided instead

eventTypeSlug.description: Event type slug, as an alternative to eventTypeId; must be combined with username (or teamSlug for team event types)

username.description: Username owning the event type, when identifying it by eventTypeSlug

teamSlug.description: Team slug owning the event type, when booking a team event type by eventTypeSlug

metadata.description: Optional key-value metadata to store on the booking

tools.name: Get Booking
tools.description: Get a single booking by UID, e.g. to confirm details before rescheduling or cancelling

bookingUid.description: UID of the booking (from list_bookings); a recurring booking UID returns all its recurrences

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-02-25 for this endpoint

tools.name: Reschedule Booking
tools.description: Move an existing booking to a new start time (only accepted or pending bookings can be rescheduled)

bookingUid.description: UID of the booking to reschedule (from list_bookings)

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-02-25 for this endpoint

start.description: New booking start time in UTC ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-06-20T10:00:00Z (find open times with get_available_slots)

reschedulingReason.description: Reason for rescheduling, shown to the organizer and attendees

rescheduledBy.description: Email of whoever initiates the reschedule; when it is the event owner's email the new time auto-confirms, otherwise the owner must confirm

tools.name: Confirm Booking
tools.description: Confirm a booking that is awaiting host approval (status unconfirmed in list_bookings)

bookingUid.description: UID of the unconfirmed booking to confirm

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-02-25 for this endpoint

tools.name: Cancel Booking
tools.description: Cancel an existing booking, optionally recording a cancellation reason

bookingUid.description: UID of the booking to cancel (from list_bookings)

cal-api-version.description: Cal.com API version header; must be 2026-02-25 for this endpoint

cancellationReason.description: Reason for the cancellation, shown to the organizer and attendees

cancelSubsequentBookings.description: For recurring bookings only: also cancel all recurrences after this one

prompts.title: Upcoming bookings
prompts.prompt: Show me my upcoming Cal.com bookings and who I'm meeting with.

prompts.title: Find an open slot
prompts.prompt: Find available time slots on my Cal.com event type for the next few days.

prompts.title: Book a meeting
prompts.prompt: Book a meeting on my Cal.com event type for an attendee at the next available slot.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Cal.com setup

steps.title: Create a Cal.com account
steps.description: Sign up for a free account at https://cal.com/signup (or use your self-hosted instance) and set up at least one event type so there is something to book.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: In Cal.com, go to Settings > Developer > API Keys and click Add. Give the key a name, optionally set an expiration, and save it.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Copy the generated key into CALCOM_API_KEY. Keys start with cal_ (test) or cal_live_ (live) and are sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

notes: Most v2 endpoints require a cal-api-version header with a fixed date value that differs per endpoint; the tools set the correct default automatically.
Booking start times must always be provided in UTC, even when the attendee's time zone differs.
API keys are rate limited to 120 requests per minute by default.
